decisions.reasoning: Stock pulled back ~10% from $236 peak to $212, position -2.41% underwater. Key near-term risk: June 11 Senate Banking Committee hearing (Sen. Warren invited CEO Jensen Huang to testify on China AI chip sales/export controls, CNBC Jun 4). DOJ probe into chip diversion also an overhang. However, fundamentals remain exceptional: 56% YoY revenue growth in Q2 FY2026 ($58.3B), consensus analyst PT $305 (+44% upside from ~$212), 51 Buy / 3 Hold / 0 Sell. Fast SMA ($216) slightly above slow SMA ($215) -- technical signal still long but price trading below both SMAs, indicating weakening momentum. Trimming here locks in losses before potential recovery post-hearing; thesis not invalidated.

sources.headline: Senator Warren invites Jensen Huang to testify before Senate Banking Committee on China AI chip sales
NVIDIA acquires predictive AI startup Kumo AI for ~$400M
NVDA consensus PT $305.67, 51 Buy / 3 Hold analyst ratings

decisions.invalidation: DOJ files formal charges or Commerce Dept materially expands export control restrictions; or stock breaks below $200 support on heavy volume

decisions.reasoning: Position essentially flat (-0.01%). WWDC 2026 runs June 8-12 -- Siri AI reveal powered by Google Gemini is the pivotal announcement (Motley Fool Jun 1). Morgan Stanley (Yahoo Finance ~Jun 1) notes low investor expectations create favorable risk/reward setup; base PT $330 (Overweight), bull case up to $440. Holding through WWDC catalyst rather than selling into flat position with asymmetric upside.

sources.headline: Apple biggest AI test arrives June 8: what is at stake at WWDC
WWDC is a key catalyst; base PT $330, low investor expectations favorable setup

decisions.invalidation: WWDC keynote disappoints on AI features (Siri demo fails or delayed); India CCI fine materializes at full $38B

decisions.reasoning: Position -0.58% underwater. Build 2026 (June 2) was positive: Office 365 Agent Mode (late June), GitHub Copilot as autonomous agent, Azure AI Foundry. June 2 selloff (-4.1%) driven by Trump voluntary AI review exec order -- low fundamental risk. Stifel downgraded to Hold citing overly optimistic FY2027 expectations, flagged as risk. Morgan Stanley reiterated PT $650 (+44% upside). Holding -- AI agent narrative intact, Q4 earnings late July is the next major catalyst.

sources.headline: Microsoft Build 2026: AI agent expansion, Morgan Stanley eyes 52% Azure upside
Why Microsoft stock is sinking today (Trump voluntary AI review exec order)

decisions.invalidation: Q4 earnings show Azure growth deceleration; FTC takes formal action on cloud/AI bundling

decisions.reasoning: Best performer in portfolio: +2.23% unrealized ($49 gain). S&P 500 hit record close 7,609 on June 2 (CNBC) before pulling back. Holding through near-term catalysts: NFP today, CPI June 10, FOMC June 16-17 (new chair Warsh debut, 98.7% probability of hold). Valuation stretched (Shiller P/E 42.78) but macro uptrend intact. No reason to trim a winning position ahead of data.

sources.headline: S&P 500 hits first close above 7,600, capping 9-day win streak
Market Quick Take: NFP today, CPI June 10, FOMC June 16-17 are key catalysts; VIX 16.06, cautiously risk-off

decisions.invalidation: NFP comes in hot (>300K or CPI surprises to upside above 4%), triggering hawkish Fed repricing

summary: No trades - held all 4 positions (NVDA/AAPL/MSFT/SPY); multiple catalysts in next 2 weeks (NVDA Senate hearing Jun 11, AAPL WWDC Jun 8, CPI Jun 10, FOMC Jun 16-17) and limited cash headroom at 92.9% gross long exposure
open_theses: NVDA: AI GPU demand secular thesis intact despite regulatory headwinds; watching June 11 Senate hearing for severity of export control language
AAPL: WWDC June 8-12 Siri/AI reveal is binary catalyst; low expectations per Morgan Stanley create asymmetric upside
MSFT: Azure AI agent monetization narrative; Q4 earnings late July is next major checkpoint
SPY: Broad market uptrend intact; watching NFP today and CPI June 10 for inflation signals ahead of FOMC June 16-17
